subject: Databases
title: British Airways IT Meltdown 2017
description: A quick win: understand British Airways IT Meltdown 2017.

hook.text: May 2017. A power supply is accidentally switched off in British Airways' data center. The UPS fails on restart.

buildup.text: The power surge when systems came back online corrupted databases and scrambled server configurations.

discovery.text: 75,000 passengers were stranded. All flights from Heathrow and Gatwick were cancelled for two days.

twist.text: BA had no effective disaster recovery. Their backup data center was in the same area, hit by the same power issue.

climax.text: The cost: £80 million in compensation plus untold reputation damage. All from one power switch.

punchline.text: DR sites must be truly independent. Same-area backups fail together.

quiz.question: Why did British Airways' disaster recovery fail?
options: The backup data center was in the same area and was affected by the same power issue
They had no backup data center at all
The disaster recovery plan worked perfectly
